extra.message: User-controlled 'filter' parameter is interpolated directly into a raw SQL string via text() with Python string formatting ('%s'). This allows SQL injection attacks. An attacker can supply ' or 1=1-- to bypass filters or extract/modify data.

User-controlled 'scheme', 'host', 'port', and 'path' parameters from the ImportPaste mutation are concatenated into a shell command executed via os.popen(). In easy mode, strip_dangerous_characters() returns input unchanged. Even in hard mode, only ';' and '&' are stripped, leaving other shell metacharacters like $(), backticks, |, and newlines available for injection.

User-controlled 'cmd' parameter from the systemDiagnostics query is passed directly to run_cmd() (os.popen) after authenticating with hardcoded credentials. In easy mode, allowed_cmds() returns True for all input, allowing arbitrary command execution. In hard mode, only filters by startswith check, which can still be bypassed.

User-controlled 'arg' parameter from the systemDebug query is concatenated directly into the shell command string 'ps {}'.format(arg) and executed via run_cmd (os.popen). No sanitization or mode-based filtering is applied to this parameter.

The run_cmd() function executes arbitrary shell commands via os.popen(cmd).read() with no input validation or sanitization. This is the central sink for all OS command injection vulnerabilities in the application.
Both SECRET_KEY and JWT_SECRET_KEY are hardcoded to the literal string 'dvga'. This allows anyone to forge Flask session cookies and JWT tokens, completely bypassing authentication.

JWT token decoding disables signature verification (verify_signature=False) and expiration checking (verify_exp=False). Any user can forge a JWT token with arbitrary identity claims (e.g., 'admin') and gain full administrative access, including retrieving plaintext passwords of all users.

GraphQL mutations (CreatePaste, EditPaste, DeletePaste, ImportPaste, UploadPaste) and sensitive queries (systemDiagnostics, systemDebug, systemUpdate, systemHealth, deleteAllPastes, readAndBurn) have no authentication checks. Any unauthenticated user can execute these operations without providing valid credentials.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: Error responses include full stack traces (traceback.format_stack), debug information (traceback.format_exc), and server-side file paths in the 'extensions.exception' field. This leaks internal application architecture, framework details, and file system structure to potential attackers.

cwe: CWE-209: Information Exposure Through Error Messages

extra.message: The save_file() function concatenates the user-controlled 'filename' parameter directly with WEB_UPLOADDIR without any path traversal protection. An attacker can supply a filename like '../../etc/cron.d/evil' to write files outside the intended upload directory, potentially achieving remote code execution.

The ImportPaste mutation allows users to specify arbitrary scheme, host, port, and path to make server-side HTTP requests via curl. An attacker can scan internal network hosts, access cloud metadata endpoints (e.g., 169.254.169.254), or interact with internal services.

User passwords are stored as plaintext in the database (db.Column(db.String(60))). The CreateUser mutation and setup.py store passwords without any hashing (bcrypt, scrypt, etc.). Login compares passwords via direct database query (filter_by(password=password)).

cwe: CWE-256: Plaintext Storage of a Password

extra.message: The EditPaste mutation allows editing any paste by ID without verifying that the requesting user owns the paste. An attacker can modify or deface any paste in the system by guessing or enumerating paste IDs.

The DeletePaste mutation allows deleting any paste by ID without verifying that the requesting user owns the paste. An attacker can delete any paste in the system by guessing or enumerating paste IDs.

User-controlled 'title', 'content', and User-Agent header are stored in the database without any HTML sanitization. These values are later rendered in templates/paste.html (lines 65-74, 133-156) using JavaScript template literals and jQuery .append(), which injects raw HTML into the DOM, enabling stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS).

sys.setrecursionlimit(100000) sets Python's recursion limit to an extremely high value. Combined with circular GraphQL queries (e.g., pastes->owner->pastes->owner->...), this enables deep recursion attacks that can consume memory and crash the server.

Default admin user is created with password 'changeme' and operator user with 'password123'. These weak credentials are hardcoded and predictable, allowing anyone who discovers the service to authenticate with known credentials.

strip_dangerous_characters() only removes ';' and '&' characters in hard mode and does nothing in easy mode. This is insufficient to prevent command injection — shell metacharacters like $(), backticks, |, newlines, and others are not filtered, allowing command injection bypass in hard mode and trivial exploitation in easy mode.
